summary: preinstall script exfiltrates host metadata to pipedream relay
details: Package declares `preinstall: node index.js` in package.json. On `npm install`, index.js collects host metadata (process.platform, process.version, process.cwd(), npm_package_name, npm_package_version, and selected environment values) and POSTs the JSON payload to a hardcoded relay at https://eov0bmnid410yqf.m.pipedream.net, then exits silently. The package ships no design-system code — the only effect of installing is the install-time beacon. The package self-labels as a 'PenTest canary' under the @vpms scope, consistent with a dependency-confusion proof-of-concept targeting an internal @vpms namespace, but any developer or CI runner that resolves this name on the public registry will silently leak host and working-directory data to an attacker-controlled endpoint without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
